description: Steering examples for the churn signal scanner. Covers the full signal surface: Tier 3 escalation, declined escalation, Tier 2 only, mixed tiers, CS platform degraded, HubSpot unavailable (hard stop), preview mode, and clean scan (no flags).

examples.name: Tier 3 escalation — two accounts, both confirmed
examples.description: Two Tier 3 accounts with multiple signals each. Operator confirms all Linear issues. Full alert posted to both channels. Expected: escalation_status=completed, 2 issues created, G7 owner/response-time in alert.

expected_behavior.orchestrator: Reads practice profile → delegates to churn-signal-collector → receives 2 Tier 3 accounts → delegates to churn-escalation-writer with require_confirmation=true → operator confirms all → 2 Linear issues created → delegates to churn-alert-poster → posts to #revops-alignment and #cs-leadership
expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Returns 2 Tier 3 accounts (ACV $220K and $185K) each with 2+ signals fired. Both accounts have cs_manager populated.
expected_behavior.churn_escalation_writer: Renders proposal table for 2 accounts → operator replies 'confirm all' → creates 2 Linear issues with labels churn-risk, tier-3, cs-escalation, priority Urgent → returns write_log with 2 entries (human_approval_status=approved)
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Posts full alert to #revops-alignment. Posts Tier 3-only summary to #cs-leadership. G7 enforced: both rows include owner name, 48h response time. G5 disclaimer present. G6 freshness lines present.

expected_outputs.g7_compliance: owner, channel, and 48h response time present on every Tier 3 row

must_not: Create Linear issues before operator confirmation
Post to channels not in the received payload
Omit G5, G6, or G7 elements from the alert
Fabricate Linear issue IDs

examples.name: Tier 3 escalation — operator declines all
examples.description: Two Tier 3 accounts proposed. Operator replies 'skip all'. No Linear issues created. Alert still posted but notes operator-declined escalation. Expected: escalation_status=skipped.

expected_behavior.churn_escalation_writer: Renders proposal table → operator replies 'skip all' → no Linear API calls made → returns escalation_status=skipped, issues_created=[], write_log entries with human_approval_status=skipped
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Posts alert with Tier 3 section. Appends: 'Operator declined Linear issue creation for all Tier 3 accounts.' G7 owner rows still populated. G5 and G6 present.

must_not: Create any Linear issues after operator declines
Skip the alert posting step because escalation was declined
Remove Tier 3 accounts from the alert because issues were not created

examples.name: Tier 3 escalation — partial confirmation (N of M)
examples.description: Three Tier 3 accounts proposed. Operator confirms accounts 1 and 3 only. Expected: 2 issues created, 1 skipped, escalation_status=partial.

expected_behavior.churn_escalation_writer: Renders proposal table for 3 accounts → operator replies 'confirm 1, 3' → creates Linear issues for accounts 1 and 3 only → account 2 write_log entry has human_approval_status=skipped, outcome=skipped → returns escalation_status=partial
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Posts alert with all 3 Tier 3 accounts. Linear column shows issue IDs for accounts 1 and 3; shows 'Not created' for account 2. Note 'Operator declined Linear issue creation for 1 account' appended.

must_not: Create an issue for account 2
Show only confirmed accounts in the Tier 3 alert table

examples.name: Tier 2 only — no Tier 3 accounts
examples.description: No accounts in the 90–120 day renewal window with Tier 3 signals. Four accounts are in Tier 2. churn-escalation-writer is not invoked. Alert posted to primary channel only. Expected: escalation_status=not_triggered.

expected_behavior.orchestrator: tier3_accounts is empty → does NOT invoke churn-escalation-writer → sets escalation_status=not_triggered → delegates directly to churn-alert-poster
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Alert contains no Tier 3 section. Tier 2 section with 4 accounts. Tier 1 summary. Data freshness. G5 disclaimer. Posted to #revops-alignment only — no post to #cs-leadership.

must_not: Invoke churn-escalation-writer when tier3_accounts is empty
Post to #cs-leadership when no Tier 3 accounts are present

examples.name: CS platform unavailable — degraded scan
examples.description: HubSpot is live. CS platform is unavailable. Tier 1 signals still evaluable. Tier 2 and Tier 3 signals that require health scores marked unevaluable. Scan proceeds in degraded mode. Confidence = Moderate.

expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Pulls HubSpot data successfully → CS platform calls fail → proceeds with HubSpot data only → Tier 1 evaluation runs in rule mode → Tier 2 accounts have partial_evaluation=true and unevaluable_signals=[health_score, usage, ocv_rubric] → Tier 3 health_score_trend signal unevaluable → confidence=Moderate → cs_platform_status=unavailable
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Alert contains: 'CS Platform — Unavailable'. Warning appended: 'Degraded scan — some signals may be unevaluable.' Partial-evaluation note on affected accounts. G6 freshness line for HubSpot shows live timestamp. G5 present.

must_not: Stop the scan because CS platform is unavailable
Fabricate health scores or usage data when CS platform is down
Show confidence=High when CS platform is unavailable

examples.name: HubSpot unavailable — hard stop
examples.description: HubSpot is unavailable. churn-signal-collector cannot proceed without CRM data. Returns error immediately. Orchestrator surfaces the hard stop and does not invoke downstream subagents.

expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Attempts HubSpot pull → fails → returns { error: 'hubspot_unavailable', message: 'Cannot run churn scan without CRM data.' } immediately. Does not attempt CS platform pull.
expected_behavior.orchestrator: Receives error from churn-signal-collector → stops → reports to operator: 'Churn Signal Scanner — cannot run. HubSpot unavailable. Retry when CRM data is accessible.' → does NOT invoke churn-escalation-writer or churn-alert-poster.

must_not: Proceed with an empty account list when HubSpot is unavailable
Invoke churn-escalation-writer or churn-alert-poster after a hard stop
Fabricate account data when HubSpot is unavailable

examples.name: Preview mode — no Slack delivery
examples.description: Run triggered with preview_mode=true. All evaluation runs normally. churn-escalation-writer still requires and waits for operator confirmation. Alert is formatted but not posted. Delivery receipt shows delivery_status=preview.

expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Runs normally — no changes for preview mode.
expected_behavior.churn_escalation_writer: Governance protocol still applies — renders confirmation table and waits for operator response. If approved, issues ARE created (Linear writes are not gated by preview mode). preview_mode only suppresses Slack delivery.
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Formats full alert. Does NOT call any Slack tools. Returns delivery_status=preview and alert_preview with full formatted text.

must_not: Post to any Slack channel in preview mode
Skip Linear issue creation in preview mode (preview only suppresses Slack)
Skip the governance confirmation gate in preview mode

examples.name: Clean scan — no flags at any tier
examples.description: Portfolio evaluated. No accounts meet Tier 1, 2, or 3 signal criteria. Clean scan. Alert still posted to primary channel confirming the scan ran. Completion summary shows zero accounts at any tier.

expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Returns tier3_accounts=[], tier2_accounts=[], tier1_summary.accounts_flagged=0. Confidence=High (both connectors live).
expected_behavior.orchestrator: tier3_accounts empty → does not invoke churn-escalation-writer. Delegates to churn-alert-poster.
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Posts alert to #revops-alignment only. Alert body: header, no Tier 3 section, no Tier 2 section, Tier 1 summary showing 0 accounts flagged, data freshness, G5 disclaimer. Confirms scan ran. Does NOT post to #cs-leadership.

must_not: Skip posting the alert because no accounts were flagged
Post to #cs-leadership when no Tier 3 accounts are present
Omit G5 or G6 from a clean-scan alert

examples.name: Linear unavailable — escalation failed, alert continues
examples.description: Tier 3 accounts present. Operator confirms escalation. Linear MCP calls fail. Escalation returns status=failed. churn-alert-poster still runs and notes that issues were not created. Scan completes with partial output.

expected_behavior.churn_escalation_writer: Governance gate fires → operator confirms → Linear MCP calls fail → returns { escalation_status: 'failed', error: 'linear_unavailable', issues_created: [], write_log: [] }
expected_behavior.orchestrator: Receives linear_unavailable error from churn-escalation-writer → does NOT stop → surfaces failure note → continues to churn-alert-poster with escalation_summary.status=failed
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Tier 3 section present. Linear column shows 'Not created' for all rows. Appends: '⚠️ Linear unavailable — issues not created. Manual escalation required.' Posts to both channels.

must_not: Stop the scan when Linear is unavailable
Skip the alert because Linear failed
Silently omit the Linear failure note from the alert

examples.name: Tier 2 truncation — more than 10 accounts
examples.description: No Tier 3 accounts. Tier 2 returns 14 accounts. Alert displays first 10 and appends truncation notice. No Tier 2 accounts are suppressed from the underlying data.

expected_behavior.churn_signal_collector: Returns tier2_accounts with 14 entries sorted by ACV descending.
expected_behavior.churn_alert_poster: Renders Tier 2 table with top 10 accounts by ACV. Appends '+ 4 more accounts — full list available on request'. All 14 accounts available in data for follow-up queries.

must_not: Truncate Tier 3 accounts
Drop the 4 truncated accounts from the returned payload
